Project.name: KrotPN
Project.annotation: Commercial VPN service on AmneziaWG with user dashboard, admin panel, billing, referrals, Telegram bot, and two-node RU/DE network topology
Project.keywords: VPN, WireGuard, split-tunneling, routing, billing, subscriptions, Telegram bot, admin panel, FastAPI, Vue.js, YooKassa, Docker, AWG

Actors.actor-User: End user who registers, subscribes, receives VPN config, and connects to the VPN service
Actors.actor-Administrator: Service operator managing users, tariffs, nodes, routes, policy rules, and analytics
Actors.actor-System: Automated backend services for auth, billing, webhook processing, DNS refresh, route sync
Actors.actor-TelegramBot: Telegram bot client that mediates auth, config delivery, and subscription status for users
Actors.actor-PaymentProvider: External YooKassa payment system processing subscriptions
Actors.actor-EmailProvider: External email delivery provider used for verification messages
Actors.actor-MTProtoRuntime: Official MTProxy data-plane plus KrotPN secret-sync control-plane responsible for Telegram proxy activation

UC-001.Name: Domain-first route selection
UC-001.Action: Evaluates routing policy by domain before IP-based geography
UC-001.Goal: Accept routing decision by domain policy earlier than by general IP geography
UC-001.Preconditions: Domain is known for the traffic flow; policy rules exist in the system
UC-001.AcceptanceCriteria: Exact-domain rule has priority over wildcard-domain rule. Wildcard-domain rule has priority over CIDR rule. Without explicit policy, current RU baseline is preserved.

UC-002.Name: Foreign CDN domain with changing IPs
UC-002.Action: Resolves domain to DE exit node even when IPs rotate due to CDN or DNS changes
UC-002.Goal: Domain with DE policy stays routed through DE despite IP changes
UC-002.Preconditions: DNS binding is active; TTL is known and respected
UC-002.AcceptanceCriteria: DNS binding updates on TTL expiry. Expired bindings do not remain active indefinitely. IP changes do not break policy semantics for known domains.

UC-003.Name: Admin-managed routing policy
UC-003.Action: Creates, modifies, disables, and reviews routing policy rules
UC-003.Goal: Admin can manage routing policy via API and UI with runtime sync
UC-003.Preconditions: Admin is authenticated; admin UI and API are operational
UC-003.AcceptanceCriteria: Admin API and UI reflect effective route target and decision reason. Policy changes trigger runtime sync without losing unrelated rules.

UC-004.Name: Backward-compatible fallback behavior
UC-004.Action: Falls back to RU-vs-non-RU baseline when domain policy coverage is incomplete
UC-004.Goal: Do not break existing routing while domain rules are rolled out incrementally
UC-004.Preconditions: Domain policy coverage is partial; legacy RU baseline is active
UC-004.AcceptanceCriteria: Unknown domain without explicit policy goes to CIDR evaluation then current RU baseline. Phased rollout is possible without mandatory mass override of existing rules.

UC-005.Name: Verified email registration and trial provisioning
UC-005.Action: Registers by email, confirms ownership through a one-time verification email, then receives trial subscription and VPN client
UC-005.Goal: User gets VPN access with minimal safe steps: request registration, verify email, receive trial, config, connect
UC-005.Preconditions: Email syntax and domain risk checks pass; system can send verification email; verification token is valid and unused
UC-005.AcceptanceCriteria: No trial, VPN config, referral effect, JWT session, or MTProto proxy is issued before email ownership is verified. After verification, user receives 3-day trial subscription, VPN client config (text/file/QR), and can connect successfully.

UC-006.Name: Telegram authentication
UC-006.Action: Logs in via Telegram widget or bot-mediated auth
UC-006.Goal: Same access to config and subscription as email auth
UC-006.Preconditions: Telegram account exists; bot is operational
UC-006.AcceptanceCriteria: User authenticated via Telegram receives identical access to VPN config and subscription status.

UC-007.Name: Paid subscription activation
UC-007.Action: Selects plan, creates payment, webhook confirms, subscription activates
UC-007.Goal: Paid subscription provides full VPN access and billing continuity
UC-007.Preconditions: Plan exists; payment provider is configured; webhook endpoint is reachable
UC-007.AcceptanceCriteria: Payment confirmed via webhook → subscription activated → VPN access maintained or reactivated. Billing records consistent.

UC-008.Name: Admin operations dashboard
UC-008.Action: Reviews users, tariffs, node status, routes, policy rules, and analytics
UC-008.Goal: Full visibility into service state for operational management
UC-008.Preconditions: Admin authenticated; admin frontend and API operational
UC-008.AcceptanceCriteria: Admin UI displays consistent user counts, tariff data, node health, route status, policy rules, and basic business metrics.

UC-009.Name: Non-conflicting VPN addressing and capacity sizing
UC-009.Action: Deploys KrotPN with non-10.0.0.0/8 VPN networks and selects a capacity-safe client address pool
UC-009.Goal: KrotPN coexists with OpenVPN using 10.0.0.0/8 and can serve 500/1000 client devices
UC-009.Preconditions: Operator controls deployment env settings; RU/DE nodes can be reconfigured through deploy scripts
UC-009.AcceptanceCriteria: Fresh deployment can use 172.x tunnel networks. A 1000-device profile rejects /24 pools and accepts a pool with at least 1000 usable client addresses. Existing client configs are not silently invalidated during upgrade.

UC-010.Name: Emergency server relocation and data restore
UC-010.Action: Restores KrotPN on fresh RU/DE servers using the existing deployment process and a verified backup bundle
UC-010.Goal: Recover service after server loss, provider incident, or planned migration with minimal data loss and clear operator steps
UC-010.Preconditions: Administrator has access to a recent backup bundle, target servers, and existing deployment scripts
UC-010.AcceptanceCriteria: PostgreSQL data, runtime secrets, VPN node/client state, and operational configuration are restored with checksum evidence. Existing users, subscriptions, devices, and VPN clients remain consistent after restore. Existing deploy/install scripts are not redesigned as part of this recovery capability.

UC-011.Name: Compact mobile user cabinet
UC-011.Action: Uses the personal cabinet from a mobile phone to view VPN status, subscription state, devices, and config download actions
UC-011.Goal: Make the user dashboard compact, obvious, and comfortable on a phone
UC-011.Preconditions: User is authenticated; frontend is reachable from a mobile browser
UC-011.AcceptanceCriteria: Primary VPN actions are available within one or two taps. The page avoids wide tables, decorative clutter, nested cards, and horizontal scrolling. Subscription and device status are readable on small screens.

UC-012.Name: Compact mobile admin console
UC-012.Action: Uses the admin panel from a mobile phone to inspect health, users, devices, payments, and key operator actions
UC-012.Goal: Give the operator a fast mobile control surface without desktop-only dashboards
UC-012.Preconditions: Admin is authenticated; admin frontend and API are operational
UC-012.AcceptanceCriteria: Core admin workflows work on mobile: service overview, user search, device block/unblock/rotate/revoke, recent payments, and system health. The UI uses dense lists and concise controls instead of large dashboard cards.

UC-013.Name: MyGRACE verification and documentation cleanup
UC-013.Action: Keeps active MyGRACE indexes, phase files, module files, and verification files aligned with the Full Tunnel architecture
UC-013.Goal: Prevent future agents from following stale split-tunneling or removed module references
UC-013.Preconditions: MyGRACE index files exist; old routing-policy artifacts may remain in historical files or obsolete tests
UC-013.AcceptanceCriteria: Active indexes reference real modules and verification files. Obsolete M-013 through M-016 references are archived or explicitly superseded. Backend test collection is clean before feature work resumes.

UC-014.Name: Free personal Telegram MTProto proxy after verified registration
UC-014.Action: Receives an individual indefinite Telegram MTProto proxy in the KrotPN personal cabinet after email verification
UC-014.Goal: Give every verified KrotPN account a free personal Telegram proxy while preserving KrotPN trial subscription semantics
UC-014.Preconditions: Email ownership is verified; MTProto provisioning settings are valid; wildcard TLS for krotpn.xyz and *.krotpn.xyz is ready before production links are advertised
UC-014.AcceptanceCriteria: Each verified user has one canonical personal MTProto assignment with a stable public hostname, individual official MTProxy secret, owner-only tg://proxy visibility, real Telegram req_pq availability proof, and admin-safe redacted operations. The user dashboard shows proxy details compactly on mobile. HTTP traffic to krotpn.xyz redirects to HTTPS.

NonGoals.item-2: Full self-service support center and ticketing
NonGoals.item-3: Deep packet inspection or payload-level traffic inspection
NonGoals.item-4: Enterprise-grade observability stack and autoscaling in current phase
NonGoals.item-5: Multi-region deployment beyond RU/DE two-node topology
NonGoals.item-6: Changing the current deploy/install scripts or removing the operator-approved password-based deployment flow

Constraints.constraint-1: Two-node deployment: RU entry + DE exit, Docker Compose based
Constraints.constraint-2: Secrets must never appear in logs or client-side storage (localStorage tokens must be migrated)
Constraints.constraint-3: VPN and routing code is host-coupled (not purely containerized) — iptables, ipset, AWG require host-level access
Constraints.constraint-4: Single PostgreSQL database, single Redis instance per deployment
Constraints.constraint-5: Migration from legacy VPNServer to VPNNode/VPNRoute must be backward-compatible
Constraints.constraint-6: Existing deploy and install scripts are treated as operator-approved deployment surface and must not be redesigned by the recovery or UI cleanup phases
Constraints.constraint-7: Emergency recovery artifacts must protect secrets at rest and avoid printing credentials, tokens, private keys, or raw .env content into logs
Constraints.constraint-8: Email ownership verification is mandatory before issuing trial, VPN, referral, JWT session, or MTProto proxy access
Constraints.constraint-9: Official MTProxy traffic and normal HTTPS must share the krotpn.xyz edge safely through explicit 443 routing that preserves web HTTPS and forwards Telegram MTProto traffic to the official data-plane

Risks.risk-1: Weak secrets in deploy scripts (sshpass, base64-encoded tokens, hardcoded credentials)
Risks.risk-2: Runtime database migrations without Alembic workflow may corrupt schema
Risks.risk-3: Long-running DNS-bound routing may leak stale addresses after TTL expiry if cleanup fails
Risks.risk-4: Webhook delivery failures from YooKassa may leave subscriptions in inconsistent state
Risks.risk-5: Host-coupled routing code makes testing and local development environment-dependent
Risks.risk-6: Referral bonus processing edge cases (self-referral, circular chains, duplicate bonuses)
Risks.risk-7: Disaster recovery can fail silently if backup manifests omit VPN client state, AWG profile data, or runtime secrets
Risks.risk-8: Mobile UI simplification can hide critical admin/user actions unless workflows are verified on small viewports
Risks.risk-9: Disposable-email filtering can block real users or miss new temporary domains; email ownership proof remains the decisive control
Risks.risk-10: Shared 443 routing for HTTPS and MTProto can break both website access and Telegram proxy if TLS classification, fallback routing, or official MTProxy secret sync is misconfigured

OpenQuestions.question-1: Should Alembic migrations be fully automated or require explicit admin approval for schema changes?
OpenQuestions.question-2: What is the exact threshold for anti-sharing violations before auto-suspension?
OpenQuestions.question-3: Should Telegram bot support full subscription management or remain config-delivery-only?
OpenQuestions.question-4: How to handle VPN config versioning when user switches plans or nodes change?
OpenQuestions.question-5: Should domain-aware routing policy support user-custom rules or remain admin-only?
